explanation: This command simulates the use of IEExec.exe to download and execute a file from a URL, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command is a simplified version with omitted quotes around the URL, which is still valid and matches the Sigma rule.
This command reorders the arguments, which is allowed by many command-line utilities and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses HTTPS instead of HTTP, which is still detected by the Sigma rule due to the 'https://' pattern.
This command omits the '-execute' flag, but the '-url' flag with a URL is still relevant for detection under the Sigma rule.
